settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

Phần chính:
- Tách toàn bộ logic lookup `bank holder` thành hook cục bộ `useBankHolderLookup(...)`, nên thân component giờ chỉ còn form state và render.
- Chuyển `watch(...)` sang `useWatch(...)` để các giá trị theo dõi rõ ràng hơn.
- Bỏ kiểu mutate trực tiếp `values.enabledWebhook`, thay bằng tạo payload mới khi submit.
- Gom các helper như `normalizeText`, `toUppercase`, `handleClose`, `canEnableWebhook`, `canLookupBankHolder` để giảm lặp và làm điều kiện dễ đọc hơn.

`npm run build` đã pass.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/VerifyOtp.tsx
+import { Input } from "@chakra-ui/react"
+import { useMutation } from "@tanstack/react-query"
+import { Button } from "components/ui/button"
+import {
+  DialogActionTrigger,
+  DialogBody,
+  DialogCloseTrigger,
+  DialogContent,
+  DialogFooter,
+  DialogHeader,
+  DialogRoot,
+  DialogTitle,
+} from "components/ui/dialog"
+import { Field } from "components/ui/field"
+import { enqueueSnackbar } from "notistack"
+import { useEffect } from "react"
+import { Controller, useForm } from "react-hook-form"
+import { useTranslation } from "react-i18next"
+import { queryClient } from "services"
+import bankAccountService from "services/bankAccount"
+
+type VerifyOtpDialogProps = {
+  bankAccountId?: number | null
+  onOpenChange: (open: boolean) => void
+  open: boolean
+}
+
+const VerifyOtpDialog = ({ bankAccountId, onOpenChange, open }: VerifyOtpDialogProps) => {
+  const { t } = useTranslation()
+  const {
+    control,
+    formState: { errors },
+    handleSubmit,
+    reset,
+  } = useForm<VerifyOtp>({
+    defaultValues: {
+      otp: "",
+    },
+  })
+
+  const closeDialog = () => {
+    reset()
+    onOpenChange(false)
+  }
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!open) {
+      reset()
+    }
+  }, [open, reset])
+
+  const invalidateBankAccountQueries = () => {
+    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["bankAccountService.getAccountBankMe"] })
+    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["bankAccountService.getAccountBank"] })
+  }
+
+  const { isPending: isVerifyPending, mutate: verifyOtp } = useMutation({
+    mutationFn: (values: VerifyOtp) => bankAccountService.verifyOtp(bankAccountId!, values),
+    onError: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar("Xác nhận OTP thất bại!", { variant: "error" })
+    },
+    onSuccess: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar("Xác nhận OTP thành công!", { variant: "success" })
+      invalidateBankAccountQueries()
+      closeDialog()
+    },
+  })
+
+  const { isPending: isResendPending, mutate: resendOtp } = useMutation({
+    mutationFn: () => bankAccountService.resendOtp(bankAccountId!),
+    onError: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar(t("send_otp_failed"), { variant: "error" })
+    },
+    onSuccess: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar(t("send_otp_success"), { variant: "success" })
+    },
+  })
+
+  const onSubmit = (values: VerifyOtp) => {
+    if (!bankAccountId) return
+    verifyOtp(values)
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <DialogRoot
+      onOpenChange={(details) => {
+        if (!details.open) {
+          closeDialog()
+        }
+      }}
+      open={open}
+      placement={"center"}
+    >
+      <DialogContent>
+        <DialogHeader>
+          <DialogTitle>{t("bank_account_verification")}</DialogTitle>
+        </DialogHeader>
+        <DialogBody>
+          <Field errorText={errors.otp?.message} invalid={!!errors.otp} label={t("otp_code")}>
+            <Controller
+              control={control}
+              name="otp"
+              render={({ field }) => (
+                <Input
+                  {...field}
+                  className="rounded-md border border-gray-300 pl-2.5"
+                  onChange={(e) => field.onChange(e.target.value.toUpperCase())}
+                  placeholder={t("placeholder.enter_otp_code")}
+                  value={field.value || ""}
+                />
+              )}
+              rules={{ required: t("placeholder.enter_otp_code") }}
+            />
+          </Field>
+        </DialogBody>
+
+        <DialogCloseTrigger onClick={closeDialog} />
+        <DialogFooter>
+          <Button disabled={!bankAccountId || isResendPending || isVerifyPending} loading={isResendPending} onClick={() => resendOtp()}>
+            {t("resend_otp")}
+          </Button>
+          <Button
+            disabled={!bankAccountId || isVerifyPending || isResendPending}
+            loading={isVerifyPending}
+            onClick={handleSubmit(onSubmit)}
+          >
+            {t("confirm")}
+          </Button>
+          <DialogActionTrigger asChild>
+            <Button className="rounded-md border-2 px-4" onClick={closeDialog}>
+              {t("cancel")}
+            </Button>
+          </DialogActionTrigger>
+        </DialogFooter>
+      </DialogContent>
+    </DialogRoot>
+  )
+}
+
+export default VerifyOtpDialog
*** End Patch

Đã cập nhật xong luồng OTP cho bank account.

Trong [CreateBankAccount.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/CreateBankAccount.tsx), sau khi tạo:
- nếu `status = PENDING` thì form tạo sẽ đóng và popup nhập OTP mở ngay
- nếu `status = DEFAULT` thì không mở popup OTP

Tôi cũng refactor [VerifyOtp.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/VerifyOtp.tsx) thành dialog dùng chung:
- `Resend OTP` gọi `POST /bank-account/:id/resend-otp`
- nhập OTP + `Confirm` gọi `POST /bank-account/:id/verify-otp`
- verify thành công sẽ đóng popup và refresh danh sách

Ở cả hai màn [BankAccount.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/BankAccount.tsx) và [BankAccountManagement.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/BankAccountManagement.tsx), tôi đã thêm nút `Xác thực tài khoản ngân hàng` cho các record có `status = PENDING` hoặc `DELETING`.

`npm run build` pass.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

import { client } from "./axios"

const getAccountBanks = (

): Promise<PaginationResponse<BankAccount>> => client.get(`/bank-account`, { params: { ...params } })

const getAccountBankMe = (
  params: { status?: BankAccountStatus } & BaseQueryParams,
): Promise<PaginationResponse<BankAccount>> => client.get(`/bank-account/me`, { params })

const getAccountBankId = (id: number): Promise<BankAccount> => client.get(`/bank-account/${id}`)

const createBankAccount = (body: CreateBankAccount): Promise<BaseResponse<BankAccount>> =>
  client.post(`/bank-account`, body)

const updateBankAccount = (id: number, body: Partial<BankAccount>): Promise<string> =>
  client.patch(`/bank-account/${id}`, body)

const deleteAccAccBankId = (id: number): Promise<null> => client.delete(`/bank-account/${id}`)

const verifyOtp = (id: number, body: VerifyOtp): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/verify-otp`, body)

const resendOtp = (id: number): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/resend-otp`)

const syncVaName = (id: number): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/sync-va-name`)

const getBankHolder = (params: GetBankHolderParams): Promise<string> =>
  client.get(`/bank-account/bank-holder`, { params })

const bankAccountService = {

  getAccountBankMe,

  getBankHolder,

  syncVaName,

}

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.message:
Đúng ý bạn: `BankAccountStatus` trong `.d.ts` không thể tự thành global runtime value được.

Lý do ngắn gọn:
- file `.d.ts` chỉ khai báo type cho TypeScript
- khi build ra browser, các khai báo đó bị xóa hết
- nên `BankAccountStatus.PENDING` chỉ chạy được nếu `BankAccountStatus` đến từ một file `.ts` thật và được `import`

Tôi đã chuyển sang cách sạch hơn:
- tạo runtime constant ở [bankAccount.ts](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/constants/bankAccount.ts)
- trong [BankAccount.d.ts](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/models/BankAccount.d.ts), `type BankAccountStatus` giờ bám theo constant đó qua `import("constants/bankAccount").BankAccountStatusValue`
- các chỗ cần runtime như [CreateBankAccount.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/CreateBankAccount.tsx), [BankAccount.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/BankAccount.tsx), [BankAccountManagement.tsx](/Users/kane/Workspace/airdata/3-payment-gateway/pgw-portal/src/views/BankAccountManagement/BankAccountManagement.tsx) giờ dùng `import { BankAccountStatus } from "constants/bankAccount"` rồi gọi `BankAccountStatus.PENDING` như bạn muốn.

`npm run build` pass.

Nếu bạn muốn, tôi có thể làm tiếp một bước nữa là gom các status khác (`CompanyStatus`, `TransactionStatus`...) sang cùng pattern này để project đồng nhất.

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- When proposing an automation with `automation_update`, prefer to make the tool call the last action in the turn. If brief user-facing prose is helpful, put it before the tool call, not after it. Avoid adding a separate closing message after the tool call unless the user specifically asked for extra explanation.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

import { client } from "./axios"

const getAccountBanks = (
  params: { status?: BankAccountStatus } & BaseQueryParams,
): Promise<PaginationResponse<BankAccount>> => client.get(`/bank-account`, { params: { ...params } })

const getAccountBankMe = (
  params: { status?: BankAccountStatus } & BaseQueryParams,
): Promise<PaginationResponse<BankAccount>> => client.get(`/bank-account/me`, { params })

const getAccountBankId = (id: number): Promise<BankAccount> => client.get(`/bank-account/${id}`)

const createBankAccount = (body: CreateBankAccount): Promise<BaseResponse<BankAccount>> =>
  client.post(`/bank-account`, body)

const updateBankAccount = (id: number, body: Partial<BankAccount>): Promise<string> =>
  client.patch(`/bank-account/${id}`, body)

const deleteAccAccBankId = (id: number): Promise<null> => client.delete(`/bank-account/${id}`)

const verifyOtp = (id: number, body: VerifyOtp): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/verify-otp`, body)

const resendOtp = (id: number): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/resend-otp`)

const syncVaName = (id: number): Promise<string> => client.post(`/bank-account/${id}/sync-va-name`)

const getBankHolder = (params: GetBankHolderParams): Promise<string> =>
  client.get(`/bank-account/bank-holder`, { params })

const bankAccountService = {
  createBankAccount,
  deleteAccAccBankId,
  getAccountBankId,
  getAccountBankMe,
  getAccountBanks,
  getBankHolder,
  resendOtp,
  syncVaName,
  updateBankAccount,
  verifyOtp,
}

export default bankAccountService

 import { BankAccountStatus } from "constants/bankAccount"

-import { LuSearch } from "react-icons/lu"

 import bankAccountService from "services/bankAccount"
@@
   const { isPending, mutate } = useMutation({
     mutationFn: (variables: { id: number }) => bankAccountService.syncVaName(variables.id),
     onSuccess: () => {
       enqueueSnackbar(t("notification.update_info_successfully"), { variant: "success" })
       queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["bankAccountService.getAccountBankMe"] })
     },
   })
+
+  const { isPending: isDeletingBankAccount, mutate: deleteBankAccount } = useMutation({
+    mutationFn: (id: number) => bankAccountService.deleteAccAccBankId(id),
+    onError: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar(t("delete_bank_account_failed"), { variant: "error" })
+    },
+    onSuccess: () => {
+      enqueueSnackbar(t("delete_bank_account_success"), { variant: "success" })
+      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["bankAccountService.getAccountBankMe"] })
+    },
+  })
@@

                   item?.status === BankAccountStatus.PENDING || item?.status === BankAccountStatus.DELETING,
               },
+              {
+                ariaLabel: "DELETE",
+                className:
+                  "rounded-md border border-red-200 bg-red-50 px-3 text-red-600 transition hover:bg-red-100",
+                isLoading: () => isDeletingBankAccount,
+                label: <LuTrash2 className="h-4 w-4" />,
+                onClick: (id) => deleteBankAccount(id),
+                visible: (item) => item?.status !== BankAccountStatus.DELETING,
+              },
             ],
             key: "action",
             label: "action",
             type: "button",
*** End Patch

Cụ thể:
- action column giờ có nút icon thùng rác đỏ
- click sẽ gọi `DELETE /bank-account/:id`
- xoá thành công/thất bại sẽ hiện snackbar

`npm run build` pass. Nếu bạn muốn, tôi có thể thêm luôn confirm dialog trước khi xoá để tránh bấm nhầm.
